calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate And Writ Of Administrative Mandate; Memorandum And Points Of Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Samuel Louis Aber, Esq. 415-864-8848 Legal Aid at Work for Petitioner.
Jacquelyn Young, Esq. 415-510-3553 Office of the Attorney General for Respondent (Zoom)
Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10609, 925-954-9888, raquel@transcriptlegal.com

Hearing is held.

ARGUED.

The Court gives preliminary remarks, comments and poses questions.
Mr. Aber presents arguments and states issues; claimants outside of job market should have state disability benefits, relief on denial of benefits, erroneous eligibility policy, interprets reading of regulation.

Ms. Young responds, no evidence of showing, no statement to establish uniform policy, cites case authority, Ms. Mani vountarily leaves job market and is disqualified.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER KARUNA MANI's Motion For Writ Of Mandate And Writ Of Administrative Mandate.

Taken Under Submission.

Both sides to submit a proposed order via contestdept302tr@sftc by Friday at the latest.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10609, 925-954-9888, raquel@transcriptlegal.com, Reported. (302/REQ)

DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Sep-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Samuel Aber, Esq. 213-878-1293 Legal Aid at work for Petitioner.
Jacquelyn Wong, Esq. Deputy Attorney General 415-510-3553 for Respondent.

ARGUED. Respondent's counsel Ms. Wong sent a timely notice to contest. Ms. Wong argues Demurrer should be sustained. Mr. Aber responds. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD, EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION.

Respondents' demurrer to petitioner's petition for writ of mandate.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, an order overruling Respondents' demurrer to Petition for Writ of Administrative Mandate and Writ of Mandate is signed. (see scanned order for details)***
